qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Arbitrary Groovy code injection via unsanitized matrix filter values evaluated by GroovyShell
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes a SHA-256 hex string padding bug (padLeft(32,'0') should be padLeft(64,'0')) in SecurityIO.groovy:622-626 (CWE-327). The finding describes a Groovy code injection via unsanitized matrix filter values in LifecycleGenerator.groovy:571 evaluated by GroovyShell.evaluate() in MultiPlatformGenerator.groovy:526. These are entirely different root causes, vulnerable code locations, and vulnerability classes.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The advisory describes a SHA-256 hex string padding bug in SecurityIO.groovy where padLeft(32,'0') should be padLeft(64,'0') for correct 64-character hex output. The only qualifying finding describes a Groovy code injection via unsanitized matrix filter values interpolated into Groovy expressions in LifecycleGenerator.groovy:571 and evaluated by GroovyShell.evaluate() in MultiPlatformGenerator.groovy:526. These are different vulnerable code locations, different root causes (cryptographic padding error vs. injection via unsanitized string interpolation), and different vulnerability classes (CWE-327 vs. code injection).
evidence: Advisory root cause: SecurityIO.groovy:622-626 padLeft(32,'0') instead of padLeft(64,'0') for SHA-256 hex output
Finding root cause: LifecycleGenerator.groovy:571 unsanitized string interpolation into Groovy expression evaluated by GroovyShell.evaluate()
Different CWE: CWE-327 (broken/risky cryptographic algorithm) vs code_injection
